title: Mark Carney’s national liberalism
gist: Yglesias argues Mark Carney's Canadian Liberals are the world's only thriving center-left party, and the durable reason is a distinctive party-to-nation relationship, not merely replicable tactics.

insights.claim: The global center-left is in broad crisis, with Mark Carney's Canadian Liberal Party standing out as the clear exception among comparable parties worldwide.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: Watch Canada as the outlier test case for whether center-left governance models can be exported elsewhere.
insights.locator: About a month ago, I found myself

insights.claim: Winning an election is a low bar for political success; the harder and more decisive test is governing a country effectively, which is where Biden and Starmer both failed despite winning.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: Judge incumbents on governing durability and approval trajectory, not just electoral wins.

insights.claim: Starmer's collapse does not debunk popularism; the popularism debate is effectively over because popularism is correct, and the open question has shifted to how to actually govern.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: Discount pundits who cite Starmer as proof popularism failed; the operative variable is governance quality.
insights.quote: the popularism debate is over and boring because popularism is obviously correct
insights.locator: I know a lot of people on Bluesky

themes.theme: Carney's durability and its non-replicable roots

insights.claim: Carney has maintained net-positive approval, converted a minority government into a majority by persuading MPs to defect to the Liberals, and is on track to win a new election despite the party governing since November 2015.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: A long-incumbent party regaining a majority signals unusual political franchise strength worth studying for governance stability.
insights.locator: Unlike them, Carney didn’t just win

insights.claim: Carney's most important advantage is structural and hard to copy: the Liberal Party has a fundamentally different relationship to Canada than most center-left parties have to their nations, whereas his tactical wins are broadly transferable.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: Structural national-identity fit, not tactics, is the durable moat for a governing party; hard for other center-left parties to import.
